steps.title: Set up the factoring
steps.content: For $x^2 + bx + c$, find two numbers that multiply to $c = -40$ and add to $b = 3$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A: $(x-4)(x+10) = x^2 + 6x - 40$ — wrong middle term.
  • B: $(x-5)(x+8) = x^2 + 3x - 40$ — matches.
  • C: $(x-8)(x+5) = x^2 - 3x - 40$ — wrong sign on $x$.
  • D: $(x-10)(x+4) = x^2 - 6x - 40$ — wrong middle term.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
